summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of an August 1982 show, highlighted by a soaring Bird Song, a monster Lazy Lightning→Supplication, and one of the finest Brent‑era first sets.
review: The performance is widely praised – Jerry’s lead work shines on Bird Song and the Lazy Lightning/Supplication sequence, while the second set builds to high‑energy climaxes with Scarlet > Fire and Lost Sailor > Saint. The SBD recording is solid and clear, with only minor vocal echo and brief audience patches that don’t detract from the listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and the soundboard quality outweigh a single mixed review, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Well‑recorded with clear mix; brief audience (AUD) patches are non‑intrusive. A slight vocal echo adds ambience, and early‑year bass thinness is less noticeable on this May‑onward SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as one of the finest Brent‑era openings, flowing into a second set that builds energy with strong transitions (especially the jam out of Saint) and a high‑octane finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Excellent playing and tone noted repeatedly.
